articles.title: Gas Prices, Iran Conflict Push Inflation in US to Three-Year Peak, PCE Data Shows
articles.description: The Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge, the PCE index, rose 4.1% annually in May, its highest level since 2023, driven largely by oil and gas price spikes tied to the Iran conflict. , US News, Times Now

articles.title: Rebounding AI stocks pull Wall Street higher
articles.description: Another veer up the roller coaster for artificial-intelligence stocks is pulling the U.S. market higher. The S&P 500 rose 0.6% Thursday to trim its loss for the week. The Dow Jones Industrial Average climbed 307 points, and the Nasdaq composite gained 0.6%. Micron Technology helped lead the market after the memory chip company reported much stronger profit and revenue for the latest quarter than analysts anticipated and gave a stronger-than-expected growth forecast. Stocks also benefited from easing Treasury yields in the bond market. They regressed after oil prices dipped and reports showed inflation is behaving pretty much as expected.
